chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْمُحْرِمِ يَحْتَجِمُ أَوْ يَقُصُّ ظُفُراً أَوْ شَعْراً أَوْ شَيْئاً مِنْهُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 95 – The one in Ihraam gets cupping done, or he clips nails, or hair, or something from it

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/319)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/319)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الْمُحْرِمِ يَحْتَجِمُ قَالَ لَا إِلَّا أَنْ لَا يَجِدَ بُدّاً فَلْيَحْتَجِمْ وَ لَا يَحْلِقْ مَكَانَ الْمَحَاجِمِ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Hammad, from Al halby who said,
‘I asked Abu Abdullahasws about the one in Ihraam getting cupping done. Heasws said: ‘No, unless it is inevitable, so let him get cupping done, and the place of the cupping should not be shaved’.
en.sarwar: 1. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Hammad from al-Halabiy who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘ Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who in the state of Ihram uses cupping (draws blood from his body). He (the Imam) said, ‘No, it is not permissible, unless there is no other choice, but the place from where blood is drawn must not be shaved.’”
